description: Atomic actions for deconstruction work. 36 primitives bridged from humanoid-ontology + 9 decon-specific primitives. Each primitive is the smallest unit of work a human (or robot) performs. Task codes are ordered sequences of these primitives.
bridgeNote: Primitives with humanoidId match humanoid-ontology/nets/physical/task-primitives.json exactly — shared vocabulary across construction and deconstruction ontologies.

primitives.name: Walk / Traverse
primitives.description: Move on foot across uneven, debris-covered surfaces. Includes stair/ramp traversal.
primitives.safetyNotes: Trip hazards from nails, debris, uneven ground

primitives.name: Ladder Climb
primitives.description: Ascend/descend ladder with or without tools in hand.

primitives.name: Two-Hand Carry
primitives.description: Carry object with both hands (1–50 kg). Sheets, lumber, fixtures.
primitives.safetyNotes: Load path awareness, pinch points on sheet goods

primitives.name: One-Hand Carry
primitives.description: Carry object with one hand (0.5–15 kg). Tools, hardware, small fixtures.

primitives.name: Lift from Ground
primitives.description: Deadlift object from ground level (5–120 kg).
primitives.safetyNotes: Proper body mechanics, team lift >50 lbs

primitives.name: Overhead Lift
primitives.description: Raise object above shoulder height (10–60 kg).
primitives.safetyNotes: Falling object hazard, hard hat required

primitives.name: Demolition Strike
primitives.description: Sledgehammer or similar impact tool to break connections. Force-adaptive — requires feel for material resistance.
primitives.safetyNotes: Eye protection, flying debris, structural awareness

primitives.name: Pry / Lever
primitives.description: Leverage-based separation of connected materials. Pry bar, flat bar, cat's paw.
primitives.safetyNotes: Controlled force direction, material preservation awareness

primitives.name: Power Saw Cut
primitives.description: Cut with powered saw — circular, reciprocating, oscillating, or chain saw.
primitives.safetyNotes: Blade guard, cut depth awareness (hidden utilities, adjacent materials)

primitives.name: Hand Cut
primitives.description: Cut with non-powered tool — hand saw, snips, utility knife.

primitives.name: Nail Gun Fasten
primitives.description: Pneumatic or cordless nailer. Used in bracing installation during decon.

primitives.name: Screw Drive
primitives.description: Impact driver or drill-driver to drive/remove screws.

primitives.name: Screw Remove
primitives.description: Reverse-drive screws to separate assemblies. Preserves both materials.
primitives.note: Decon-specific — construction ontology fastens, decon ontology unfastens

primitives.name: Nail Extract
primitives.description: Remove nails with cat's paw, flat bar, or nail puller. Flush-cut with oscillating tool when extraction would damage material.
primitives.note: Decon-specific. Flush-cut vs extract decision = material preservation skill

primitives.name: Manual Fasten
primitives.description: Hammer, wrench, or hand tool fastening.

primitives.name: Drill
primitives.description: Create holes with drill or impact driver. Includes core drilling for concrete.

primitives.name: Measure
primitives.description: Tape measure, laser measure, or ruler to determine dimensions.

primitives.name: Mark
primitives.description: Pencil, chalk line, or spray paint to indicate cut lines, lift points, or staging positions.

primitives.name: Visual Inspection
primitives.description: Assess condition, quality, structural integrity, or material type by sight.
primitives.note: Recovery rate depends on inspection quality — knowing what to save vs discard

primitives.name: Plumb Check
primitives.description: Verify vertical alignment with level or plumb bob.

primitives.name: Level Check
primitives.description: Verify horizontal alignment.

primitives.name: Position Hold
primitives.description: Hold component in position during fastening, cutting, or rigging. Extremely high robot value — frees human hands.

primitives.name: Sweep
primitives.description: Broom sweeping of debris, dust, nails.

primitives.name: Dump / Deposit
primitives.description: Load debris into container, dump to staging area or waste stream.

primitives.name: Stack / Organize
primitives.description: Sort and arrange materials by type, size, or destination zone.
primitives.note: Directly feeds BOH zone staging. Sort quality = resale value

primitives.name: Material Sort
primitives.description: Classify material into recovery streams: resale, reuse, recycle, waste. Core decon skill.
primitives.note: HIGH robot ROI — repetitive classification task with visual + weight signals

primitives.name: Wipe / Clean Surface
primitives.description: Surface cleaning of recovered materials for resale preparation.

primitives.name: Roll Material
primitives.description: Deploy or retrieve roll goods — housewrap, underlayment, insulation batts.

primitives.name: Dig / Excavate
primitives.description: Shovel, post-hole digger, or hand excavation.

primitives.name: Pour
primitives.description: Controlled liquid pour — water for dust suppression, concrete for backfill.

primitives.name: Shovel Lift
primitives.description: Scoop and lift with shovel — gravel, soil, debris.

primitives.name: Hazmat Sample
primitives.description: Controlled collection of hazardous material sample with sealing protocol. Asbestos, lead paint, mold.
primitives.safetyNotes: PPE Level C minimum, sealed container, chain of custody

primitives.name: Hazmat Containment
primitives.description: Seal and maintain containment of hazardous materials. Poly sheeting, negative pressure.

primitives.name: Lockout / Tagout
primitives.description: Energy isolation protocol for electrical, gas, water, and mechanical systems.
primitives.safetyNotes: Must be verified by licensed professional (electrical, plumbing)

primitives.name: Floor Sand / Push
primitives.description: Floor sander operation or push-type material movement.

primitives.name: Staple
primitives.description: Staple gun for vapor barrier, poly sheeting, or temporary fastening.

primitives.name: Crane Rigging
primitives.description: Attach slings, shackles, spreader bars to load for crane lift. Includes weight estimation, center-of-gravity assessment, and lift point selection.
primitives.safetyNotes: Certified rigger required. Load must be secured before signal.
primitives.note: Decon-specific — construction uses crane but rigging is decon's core choreography

primitives.name: Crane Signal
primitives.description: Hand signals or radio communication to direct crane operator. Standard OSHA hand signals.
primitives.safetyNotes: Designated signal person only. Clear line of sight to operator.

primitives.name: Section Cut
primitives.description: Strategic structural cuts to divide roof, wall, or floor into liftable sections. Requires structural awareness — cut sequence matters.
primitives.safetyNotes: Structural engineer sign-off on cut plan. Never cut last bearing connection before rigging.
primitives.note: Decon-specific — the defining skill of crane-based deconstruction

primitives.name: Flush-Cut Nail
primitives.description: Cut nail flush with material surface using oscillating tool rather than extracting. Preserves sheet goods (sheathing, subfloor) when ring-shank or spiral nails resist extraction.
primitives.note: Decon-specific. Key material preservation technique — extraction damages the sheet, flush-cut preserves it.

primitives.name: Core Drill (Concrete)
primitives.description: Diamond core drill to create lifting holes in concrete panels. 2-inch minimum for through-bolt + shackle rigging.
primitives.safetyNotes: Water cooling, dust extraction, rebar location scan before drilling

primitives.name: Hydraulic Break
primitives.description: Brokk 170 or Track Breaker hydraulic impact to fracture concrete at controlled locations. Bond-breaking at footing joints, slab sectioning.
primitives.note: Decon-specific. Brokk is already a robot — highest robot feasibility. Track Breaker extends this to wall-mounted precision.

primitives.name: Magnetic Sweep
primitives.description: Pass magnetic sweeper over ground to collect loose nails, screws, metal debris. Critical safety step after every phase.
primitives.safetyNotes: Required after roof, wall, and floor phases. Protects crew, tires, and future site use.
primitives.note: Decon-specific. High robot feasibility — repetitive, spatial, safety-critical.

primitives.name: Temporary Brace
primitives.description: Install temporary bracing to maintain structural integrity during sequential removal. Walls braced before floor removal, top floor braced before roof removal.
primitives.safetyNotes: Structural sequencing — brace BEFORE removing the element that provides current support.
primitives.note: Decon-specific. Construction installs permanent structure; decon must temporarily replace what it's removing.

primitives.name: Utility Disconnect
primitives.description: Physically disconnect electrical, gas, water, or sewer connections. Precedes all structural work.
primitives.safetyNotes: Licensed professional required for gas and electrical. Verification test after disconnect.

cutting.note: Power saw, hand, drill, section cut, core drill

fastening.note: Nail gun, screw, manual, staple — used in bracing

unfastening.note: Screw remove, nail extract, flush-cut — decon core
